rationale: Gate-integrity re-attack. All 4 of my round-1 asks ADDRESSED. (1) Reviewer floor: R5 now bounds panel.reviewers to count>=1 AND sum>=2, explicitly closing the reviewers:[{claude,1}]->"n-1"->0 path, and the refusals AC (TestLoad_RefusesUnweakenableKnobs) asserts both count<1 and sum<2 error. (2) Third defense: R8 names the gate.go guard `f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0` as the load-bearing third layer with an explicit do-not-delete note; TestPanelGateDecision_...DoesNotAlterDecision pins a resolved-0 panel still Blocks, and TestApproveThreshold_...RecordedExpr pins record-side 0->N-1. Verified in tree: guard is live at gate.go:257, ApproveThreshold single-homed at panel.go:76, internal/panel imports no internal/config. (3) ADR-0037 wording now says 'extension' of S3 in both the ADR Touchpoint and R7, explicitly *not* 'semantically unchanged'; SS6/8 unchanged, absent field byte-identical N-1. (4) Inert-until-consumed stated in R4 and rendered as 'declared, not yet enforced' in R9, AC-checked. Re-attack: no remaining always-pass combination -- every threshold-derivation path floors at 1 (config-side) or falls back to N-1 (record-side), sum>=2 makes "n-1">=1, and the gate guard Blocks any 0. Approve.
findings: ASK 1 (reviewers floor) ADDRESSED: R5(b) requires every reviewers[].count>=1 and sum(reviewers.count)>=2, rejected with a recovery line; the single-reviewer sum==1 -> "n-1" -> 0 hole is named and closed. Scope line and TestLoad_RefusesUnweakenableKnobs both list the count<1 and sum<2 refusals. This is the config-side analogue of R7's record-side [1,N] floor; the 'closed both sides' claim is now true.

ASK 2 (gate.go guard as third defense) ADDRESSED: R7 names the record-side fallback ('recorded 0 can never turn the gate into an always-pass'); R8 adds the 'Third-defense pin' naming `f.Res.Approves >= threshold && threshold > 0` with an explicit warning not to delete the `threshold > 0` clause trusting R5/R7. Pinned by two ACs. Verified live: gate.go:257 matches the quoted guard exactly; ApproveThreshold single home at panel.go:76; go list -deps confirms internal/panel does not import internal/config.

ASK 3 (ADR-0037 wording) ADDRESSED: ADR Touchpoints and R7 both state the amendment records approve_threshold as an EXTENSION of S3's threshold rule (in-range integer overrides the N-1 default, still single-homed, N-1 when absent), explicitly disavowing 'semantically unchanged', while keeping the accurate SS6/8-unchanged and absent-field-byte-identical claims.

ASK 4 (inert annotations) ADDRESSED: R4 states every loop: key -- like models:/runner: -- is INERT until a spec-110+ consumer enforces it (four approve gates stay human-invoked regardless of gate_authority); R9 renders models:/loop:/runner: as 'declared, not yet enforced'; TestConfigShow_EmitsPanelModelsLoop asserts the annotation. A surfaced gate_authority can no longer read as an active delegation.

RE-ATTACK (no remaining hole): The triple defense is now complete and documented. Config-side: literal approve_threshold validated to [1,sum], sum>=2, count>=1, so no config path stamps a <=0 threshold and "n-1" resolves to >=1. Record-side: any recorded 0/negative/>N falls back to N-1. Gate-side: threshold>0 guard Blocks a resolved 0. The config<->record boundary (config bounds against sum(reviewers.count), interpret bounds against recorded ExpectedReviewers) is safe in both directions -- a stamped threshold exceeding a substituted-down ExpectedReviewers falls back to the stricter N-1, never weakens.

ADVISORY (non-blocking): Scope line 65 says Load backfill is 'absent/empty -> default', so an explicit `reviewers: []` (sum 0) would backfill to 3+3 rather than be caught by the sum>=2 floor. This is fail-SAFE (you get the strong 3+3 default, never a weak panel), so it does not weaken any gate; the only real trigger for the sum>=2 refusal is a non-empty short list like [{claude,1}], which is caught. Worth a one-word precision note in plan (does empty backfill or reject) but not gate-blocking.

ADVISORY (non-blocking, blessed round 1): A recorded in-range threshold of 1 with 6 reviewers (1-of-6) is a weaker-than-default gate, but it is a deliberate, floored-at-1 (never always-pass), config-surfaced, panel.json-recorded operator choice per Non-Goals. Not a loophole.
